title: Dosarele Trumpstein și ce spun ele despre elitele lumii
summary: The article uses the Epstein files (rebranded 'Trumpstein files') to analyze how global elites compromise themselves through sexual misconduct, enabling blackmail and loyalty networks. It contrasts Europe's accountability responses (resignations, investigations) with America's denial and obstruction, arguing Western civilization's survival depends on European institutional resilience.
talking_points: Antisocial personality disorder (ASPD) is disproportionately prevalent among top leaders and managers, reaching up to 20%, explaining elite misconduct
The Epstein blackmail network, possibly operated on behalf of Moscow and/or Tel Aviv, trapped elites who let primal instincts override rational risk assessment
The logic of loyalty through compromise — promoting the incompetent or corrupt to ensure dependence — was perfected by communist systems like the NKVD and persists today in Romanian politics
Europe has responded to the Epstein revelations with real consequences (Prince Andrew, Peter Mandelson, Norwegian PM immunity lifted), while the US has produced only denials and obstruction
Romania mirrors the American pattern of sweeping corruption under the rug rather than following Europe's accountability model
author: Andrei Colțea
